sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 19.23 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.19)

statement.latex: For a group$G$, let$_{1}(G)$be the normal closure of all torsion elements of$G$, and then by induction let$_{i+1}(G)$be the inverse image of$_{1}(G/ _{i}(G))$. The torsion length of$G$is defined to be either the least positive integer$l$such that$G/ _{l}(G)$is torsion-free, or$$if no such integer exists (since$G/ _{i}(G)$is always torsion-free). Does there exists a finitely generated, or even fini...
